measurement_note: Size is W, the Warehouse inventory row count. The legal 32/128/512 tiers give every row a distinct warehouse name and the same unit-volume product, so all W groups must be emitted. The remotely Accepted join-and-group query and an independent precomputed-unit-volume aggregation have an O(W log W) portable upper bound. A correct query that derives each warehouse and uses a correlated subquery to rescan all inventory rows performs O(W^2) work, completes every tier, and is rejected only by scaling.
